seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Taylors Beach and Teddington. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Taylors Beach vs Teddington: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Taylors Beach and Teddington.
heading: Taylors Beach vs Teddington — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Taylors Beach, Queensland and Teddington, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Taylors Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Teddington (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Taylors Beach or Teddington safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Taylors Beach has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Teddington scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Taylors Beach scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Teddington scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Taylors Beach or Teddington?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Taylors Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.12% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
